event: Mystic Marriage of Saint Catherine with the Madonna and Child and Saints
symbols: ring placed on Catherine's hand — the mystic marriage, symbolizing total spiritual union with Christ
infant Christ giving the ring — Christ as divine bridegroom choosing his bride
lion at the feet of the elderly saint — attribute of Saint Jerome
book — sacred Scripture and theological wisdom
armor — the soldier of Christ and defender of the faith
golden dress of Catherine — royal dignity and the bride adorned for her husband
Mary enthroned with Child — the Incarnation as the center of the communion of saints
description: A richly colored painting showing the Virgin Mary seated centrally in a red robe and dark green mantle, holding the infant Jesus on her lap. The Christ Child reaches toward a young woman in an elegant golden dress at the right, appearing to place a ring on her finger. To the lower left, an elderly bearded man with bare chest reads from a large book, with a small lion at his feet. Behind Mary, an armored knight holds a staff, and another elderly bearded figure with a book stands to the upper right. The scene is set against a dark background, with warm, glowing colors illuminating the figures.
theological_description: The Mystic Marriage of Saint Catherine represents the theology of spiritual union between Christ and the believing soul. While depicting a post-biblical saint's vision, the theme draws deeply from scriptural imagery of Christ as Bridegroom. The infant Christ placing a ring on Catherine's finger echoes the bridal theology of Hosea 2:19-20: 'I will betroth you to me forever; I will betroth you to me in righteousness and in justice, in steadfast love and in mercy.' Paul develops this further in 2 Corinthians 11:2, presenting the Church as a pure virgin betrothed to Christ, and Ephesians 5:25-32 describes Christ's love for the Church as a husband's self-sacrificial love for his bride. The surrounding saints — Jerome with his lion and book, the armored warrior saint — represent the communion of saints who share in this covenant relationship. The entire composition is a sacra conversazione, a sacred conversation that transcends time, uniting figures from different eras in the eternal present of heavenly worship. The Incarnation at the center — God becoming a child to wed himself to humanity — is the theological foundation of all mystic marriage imagery.

scripture_refs.reason: Christ loved the Church and gave himself up for her — the bridal theology underlying the mystic marriage
Paul betrothed the Church as a pure virgin to Christ — the apostolic foundation of spiritual marriage imagery
God's covenant as betrothal — the Old Testament root of divine-human bridal union
The marriage supper of the Lamb — the eschatological fulfillment of Christ's union with his people
The Word became flesh — the Incarnation at the heart of the image, making divine-human union possible

_meta.title: Mystic marriage of Saint Catherine of Alexandria and Saint Catherine of Siena
